clips.title: Share Extension target
clips.bodyText: Share Extension receives selected text, URLs, and web page property lists. It enqueues clips through ClipPipeline and must not link MLX, ModelStore, SwiftData, or retrieval engines.

clips.title: App Group queue
clips.bodyText: The App Group queue stores one JSON clip per pending file. Enqueue writes a temporary file first, moves it atomically into pending, and uses failed quarantine for unreadable items.

clips.title: Digest trigger paths
clips.bodyText: ClipDigest runs from app launch, foreground activation, Darwin clip-enqueued notifications, and a registered BGProcessing task. Registration happens at launch, while foreground digest is the reliable fallback.

clips.title: URL extraction
clips.bodyText: URL clips are fetched only by the app-side digest service with a bounded timeout. Article extraction removes script, style, navigation, and prefers article or main content before paragraph-density fallback.

clips.title: Paragraph chunker
clips.bodyText: ParagraphChunker is pure RAGCore logic. It splits by blank-line paragraphs first, greedily merges near 800 characters, keeps 120 characters of overlap, and hard-splits overlong paragraphs.

clips.title: Apple embedding
clips.bodyText: AppleContextualEmbedding uses NaturalLanguage contextual embedding as the zero-download local path. It preserves input order, returns explicit unavailable errors when assets are missing, and never downloads from the network.

clips.title: Qwen embedding
clips.bodyText: Qwen3EmbeddingMLX uses local Qwen3-Embedding model payloads through MLX. It reports not-downloaded, simulator-unavailable, load-failed, or invalid-output errors instead of returning synthetic vectors.

clips.title: SQLite vector index
clips.bodyText: SQLiteVectorStore stores chunks and vectors in a statically linked sqlite-vec vec0 table. It records embedding engine id and dimension so incompatible embeddings force a rebuild.

clips.title: FTS5 keyword index
clips.bodyText: FTS5KeywordIndex uses the same SQLite database with the trigram tokenizer. It supports Chinese substring keyword search, deterministic ordering, and deleteClip cleanup.

clips.title: Hybrid retrieval
clips.bodyText: HybridRetriever embeds the question, queries dense vector and sparse keyword indexes, then fuses both rankings with reciprocal rank fusion. The default path takes top 20 from each side and returns 8 citation chunks.

clips.title: Ask citations
clips.bodyText: Ask retrieves local chunks before generation, builds numbered citation prompts like [1] through [8], trims context with countTokens, renders citation cards, and routes taps to Memory with clipID and chunkID.

clips.title: Zero hit behavior
clips.bodyText: When retrieval has zero hits or a low fused score, Ask short-circuits without loading or generating from the LLM. The user-facing message is 你的剪藏里没有这条.

clips.title: Bench metrics
clips.bodyText: Bench uses a fixed prompt suite, three rounds, median aggregation, first-token latency, tokens per second, output tokens, peak memory, thermal state, and Markdown export for README evidence.

clips.title: Settings model management
clips.bodyText: Settings manages active engine, active model, generation temperature, topP, maxTokens, downloaded model rows, delete actions, and explicit download failure states without pretending progress.

clips.title: Device memory gate
clips.bodyText: DeviceCapability recommends a launch model from physical memory. The 4B model targets larger-memory devices, while smaller devices use the 1.7B model and avoid out-of-memory runs.

clips.title: Offline mode
clips.bodyText: Engram's M2 loop is designed for airplane mode after clips are indexed. Local embeddings, SQLite vector search, FTS5 keyword search, and on-device generation should answer with citations without network access.

clips.title: Queue corruption handling
clips.bodyText: Corrupt or unreadable queue files move to failed quarantine with an error sidecar. The digest pipeline skips poisoned files, keeps valid queue files moving, and exposes retryable failure state.

clips.title: Memory UI
clips.bodyText: Memory shows clip timeline rows with title, source URL, note, body text, indexed or failed state, index preview, retry for failed clips, and detail navigation for citation destinations.

clips.title: Share extension memory limit
clips.bodyText: The Share Extension is intentionally lightweight because extension memory is tight. It links only ClipCore and ClipPipeline directly, completes quickly, and leaves URL fetching, indexing, and MLX work to the app.

clips.title: Local privacy
clips.bodyText: Engram stores memory traces on the user's device. Product scope excludes cloud backend, accounts, sync, API keys, and generic server chat so clip memory remains physically local.

chunks.text: Share Extension target receives selected text, URLs, and web page property lists. It enqueues clips through ClipPipeline and must not link MLX, ModelStore, SwiftData, or retrieval engines.
App Group queue stores one JSON clip per pending file. Enqueue writes a temporary file first, moves it atomically into pending, and sends unreadable items to failed quarantine.

ClipDigest trigger paths are app launch, foreground activation, Darwin clip-enqueued notification, and BGProcessing. BGProcessing registration happens at launch, while foreground digest is the reliable fallback.
URL extraction belongs only to the app-side digest service. It uses a bounded timeout, removes script style navigation, prefers article or main content, and falls back to paragraph density.
ParagraphChunker is pure RAGCore logic. It splits blank-line paragraphs first, greedily merges near 800 characters, keeps 120 characters of overlap, and hard-splits overlong paragraphs.
AppleContextualEmbedding uses NaturalLanguage contextual embedding as the zero-download local path. It preserves input order and reports unavailable local assets without network downloads.
Qwen3EmbeddingMLX uses local Qwen3-Embedding payloads through MLX. It reports not-downloaded, simulator-unavailable, load-failed, and invalid-output errors instead of synthetic vectors.
SQLiteVectorStore stores chunks and vectors in statically linked sqlite-vec vec0 tables. The schema records embedding engine id and vector dimension to force rebuilds for incompatible embeddings.
FTS5KeywordIndex shares the SQLite database and uses the trigram tokenizer. It enables Chinese substring keyword search, deterministic order, and deleteClip cleanup.

HybridRetriever embeds each question, queries dense vector and sparse keyword indexes, then fuses both rankings with reciprocal rank fusion. It takes top 20 from each side and returns 8 citation chunks.

Ask retrieves local chunks before generation, builds numbered citation prompts [1] through [8], trims context with countTokens, renders citation cards, and routes citation taps to Memory by clipID and chunkID.
Zero-hit or low-score retrieval short-circuits Ask without loading or generating from the LLM. The fixed user-facing message is 你的剪藏里没有这条.
Bench runs a fixed prompt suite for three rounds and aggregates medians for first-token latency, tokens per second, output tokens, peak memory, thermal state, and Markdown export.
Settings manages active engine, active model, generation temperature, topP, maxTokens, downloaded model rows, delete actions, and explicit download failure states without pretending progress.
DeviceCapability recommends a launch model from physical memory. The 4B model targets larger-memory devices, while smaller devices use the 1.7B model and avoid out-of-memory runs.
Offline mode works after clips are indexed. Local embeddings, SQLite vector search, FTS5 keyword search, and on-device generation answer with citations without network access.
Corrupt or unreadable queue files move to failed quarantine with an error sidecar. The digest pipeline skips poisoned files, keeps valid queue files moving, and exposes retryable failure state.
Memory UI shows timeline rows with title, source URL, note, body text, indexed or failed state, index preview, retry for failed clips, and detail navigation for citation destinations.
Share Extension memory is tight, so the extension links only ClipCore and ClipPipeline directly, completes quickly, and leaves URL fetching, indexing, and MLX work to the app.
Local privacy means Engram stores memory traces on the user's device. Product scope excludes cloud backend, accounts, sync, API keys, and generic server chat.
